text:
There was an old oak tree in the backyard. Every autumn leaves would settle around its roots. Everyone knew about this tree because it was the wise grandmother of the neighborhood.

One year, the tree fell down. The entire neighborhood was saddened. They mourned the loss as though this tree had lived centuries. But then they realized something unusual.

As new saplings took root beside the stump, children played games under its shade. Birds nested there. Every spring, flowers bloomed from its hollow. It may not have been a magnificent oak anymore, but it had plenty of life.

Eventually, the roots grew strong enough to break through the surface, and the tree was reborn. It was a smaller oak now, but there it stood, full of stories of youth, laughter, and wisdom.

The tree became a symbol of enduring life and the magic of renewal. Every spring, the neighborhood children would visit it, marveling at how it had grown from a humble stump, much like themselves. It
